title: camissonia pygmaea
intro: Camissonia pygmaea, also known as the pygmy bedstraw or dwarf wild mignonette, is a small, annual plant that belongs to the Evening Primrose family (Onagraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The plant's medicinal properties are said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Camissonia pygmaea is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is used topically to soothe skin irritations and promote healing.
